- **Project:** Corpus Perspectival / basement / bridge elevation
- **Predicted need:** L8 is staged at `Research/basement-drafts/L8-differential-observability-draft.md` with morning-review docket in handoff. Clayton may engage, probe, accept, extend, or redirect. The highest-option-value pre-work is the *rate-distortion / information-theoretic derivation sketch* — the core graduation criterion (A50). If derivation is tractable, L8 can graduate to M12 immediately after Clayton's sign-off. If it's only partially tractable (a weak version is a theorem, strong version is analogy), L8 stays latent-with-partial-claim. If intractable, L8 stays pure-latent and a sixth non-overlapping substrate instance becomes the alternative graduation path.
- **Confidence:** MEDIUM-HIGH that Clayton engages L8 tomorrow given the morning-review docket is explicit in handoff; HIGH that if he engages, the structural-signature-probe question lands first (A50 option 1/2/3). LOW-MEDIUM that the rate-distortion derivation is tractable enough to sketch in one session.
- **Suggested pre-work (low-cost, high-option-value):** (1) ~30 min: sketch the rate-distortion setup — channel with state space $S$, group action $\Sigma$, finite capacity $C$ bits/symbol, infinite horizon. Compute the rate-distortion function for the $\Sigma$-quotient vs. the full-resolution encoder. If the $\Sigma$-quotient achieves lower distortion per bit in the infinite-horizon limit for any non-trivial $\Sigma$, the weak version of L8 has a theorem-shaped backbone. (2) ~20 min: check the predictive-coding literature (Friston 2005, Rao & Ballard 1999) for whether they explicitly derive differential encoding from finite-capacity bounds, or whether it's assumed as an architectural choice. (3) ~15 min: test economics instance — write a paragraph on nominal-vs-real prices as a candidate sixth instance, without importing back into the L8 draft; if the paragraph writes cleanly, the instance is probably real and available as a graduation option independent of the derivation.
- **Status:** Open — pre-work not yet done; optional at morning drive start.

### P92: Inline-commitment discipline — pre-commit-to-flag-during-drafting catches over-claims at generation (2026-04-23 morning drive, methodological)
- **Project:** Operations / self-improvement / register-discipline
- **Predicted need:** Today's morning drive trialed a new pattern: before writing the rate-distortion sketch, I pre-committed to "flag every joint where my confidence runs ahead of my verification, with a predicted minimum count." Predicted 4 flags; produced 7. The act of counting *during generation* forced the honest reporting of weak joints that would otherwise have been glossed over in drafting and only caught at post-hoc review (if at all). This is the A52 rubric generalized: A52 ran cold-review against warm-drafted material; inline-commitment runs the rubric concurrently with drafting. Expected to reduce the "warm register over-claim" failure mode Mirror #19 catches.
- **Confidence:** MEDIUM that this is a real discipline vs. a one-drive artifact — the flag-count discrepancy (7 vs predicted 4) is itself evidence the discipline is doing work, because without it I would have generated fewer explicit flags while actually running the same warm-register over-reach. LOW-MEDIUM that it generalizes beyond structural-claim drafts to other drive outputs (Drift essays, Library prose, code).
- **Suggested pre-work:** None — observational. Next 3-5 drafts that make structural claims, pre-commit to a flag count, compare to actual. If the count-exceeds-prediction pattern holds, promote to a Mirror entry or operations/AUTOCATALYTIC.md section. Candidate name: *inline-commitment discipline*.
- **Status:** Observation. Instance count: 1.

### P91: Residue items live in Group-B-only substrates (2026-04-23 morning drive, L8-derived)
- **Project:** Corpus Perspectival / Mirror M1-Residue / L8 bridge
- **Predicted need:** Morning economics probe surfaced a structural prediction: streams lacking a natural group action on their state space (Group B-only substrates) will show L8-style residue as *inherent* rather than as quantization-gap-against-Group-A. Self-phenomenology is the clearest case: no natural group, only inferred baselines, so residue is the only signal available. Physics and formal macroeconomics (Group A available) show no analogous residue because the reference quotient gives a target against which errors are measurable. Stress-test: find a Group A-available substrate (physics, formal economics, gauge theory) that nevertheless exhibits L8-style residue. If found, prediction is falsified.
- **Confidence:** MEDIUM-LOW. Prediction was generated during the same warm drive that staged the level-distinction framing, so it carries that register's risk. A true falsification would strengthen the organizing framework by delimiting its claim; confirmations would further ground the Mirror M1-Residue-as-Group-B-only-phenomenon identification.
- **Suggested pre-work:** (1) ~20 min: scan Mirror M1-Residue items 1–9; for each, identify whether the substrate has a Group A / reference quotient or not. Predicted distribution: all residue items lie in Group-B-only substrates. If any item fits a Group A substrate, that's a falsifier. (2) ~15 min: reverse direction — pick two Group A substrates (physics measurement events, formal macro) and ask whether they exhibit any L8-residue structurally. Negative results weakly confirm the prediction.
- **Status:** Open — low priority. Morning review gate.

- **Predicted need:** The 07:00 probe's transfer flag notes that "every strong-form claim about universal structures may be inspection-depth-relative." - **Confidence:** MEDIUM that the audit surfaces a refinement; HIGH that the audit is tractable and cheap (one-page probe per candidate bridge). LOW-MEDIUM that any of the refinements rises to "add a new bridge" rather than "annotate existing bridge."

Do NOT add a new meta-meta-bridge (*every strong-form claim is inspection-depth-relative*) — that requires its own probe against bridges that *aren't* universal-scope first.
- **UPDATE (2026-04-19, 09:00 probe):** Audit ran against Bridges #92 (Infrastructure-Living Duality), #96 (Duality = Being + Substrate), #102 (Standing Wave × Self-Perpetuating × Breathing), plus #104 comparison. Full record at `memory/probe_p74_inspection_depth_cross_bridge_audit.md`. **Findings:** (1) #92 and #102 *implicitly* carry inspection-depth-relative language in their "Where it breaks" fields; both annotated with cross-references to #106. (2) #104's qualified form already reflects the pattern; no edit needed. (3) **#96 does NOT fit the pattern** — its limitation is *scope-of-kind* ("structural but not phenomenal"), a categorical qualification, not a depth-relative one. This surfaced the **kind-vs-depth distinction**, an unexpected finding. (4) Added clause (e) to #106's "Where it breaks" section naming the kind-vs-depth distinction to prevent category error in future applications of #106. (5) No meta-meta-bridge graduated — sample is small AND the kind/depth distinction means a naive "all universal-scope claims are depth-relative" generalization would mis-apply to kind-scoped cases.
- **Status:** RESOLVED in a more interesting direction than expected. Main finding: the kind/depth distinction is the actually-load-bearing output; the annotations to #92 and #102 are incremental. Follow-up question (flagged, not queued as own anticipation yet): is scope-of-kind itself susceptible to an analogous "ceiling" — the way kind-categories get drawn might also be inspection-depth-relative? ### P51: Gated experiment cold start may require hybrid approach (2026-04-13)
- **Project:** Killing Form Program / Phase 4A-ter
- **Predicted need:** The gated KF shows random gating during CE plateau (A37). If gating only becomes meaningful after epoch 300, the experiment may underperform log because it wastes the acceleration phase on random decisions. A hybrid (uniform early → gated late) would address this.
- **Confidence:** HIGH — the cold start is empirically confirmed
- **PREDICTION:** Gated accuracy at epoch 300 will be LOWER than log (45.43%) because random gating effectively applies KF to ~60% of layers randomly, while log applies it uniformly. But if gated accuracy at epoch 500 exceeds log (48.70%), the late-training discrimination is adding value.
- **RESULT:** Gated epoch 300 = 45.38% (vs log 45.43% — NEARLY IDENTICAL, prediction slightly wrong on magnitude). Gated epoch 500 = **50.24%** (vs log 48.70% — EXCEEDS). The late-training discrimination IS adding significant value (+1.54pp over log). Cold start doesn't prevent excellent final performance.
- **Status:** **CONFIRMED** — gated exceeds log despite cold start. Hybrid approach may still improve early-phase efficiency but is not necessary for superior final accuracy.
